text:
I knit a lot of scarves but wasn't putting fringes on them. So when my mother asked me to put a fringe on her new scarf I knew I needed a crochet hook. This size L hook works well if you have been using size 8 knitting needles. If you use smaller knitting needles you'd need to adjust the size accordingly. I'm happy with this purchase and might even take up some crochet projects like an afghan. So much wool so little time.

~The Rebecca Review

I purchased the black handle pair with the mandrel. It stated the pliers went up to a 9.mm, it's actually a 10mm and some of the others sizes were off too. I checked them with a caliper to make sure, and what they say the pliers numbers are as advertised is not right. There were also some nicks on part of the metal and that won't work for having a smooth circle with wire. They just don't look very well made, some metal edges are not clean and smooth. After the pliers I didn't bother checking the mandrel. With the  quality and numbers being off, I returned them.
My wife and I like these scissors. We now have plenty to spread around the house. Work well.
the wash barrel came with a hole in it, and I think they could easily spring a  leak from stress and wear on the thin material, just from collapsing and opening it

These little half wooden beans are so versatile.

Some things to know about them;

1. They are all the same color and size.

2. None of them are broken, discolored or in any way different from each other.

3. They are not hollow.

4. Back side of each little half bead (dome) is flat.

5. They are perfect for painting and sealing or dying.

6. Each half bead dome is super lightweight and easily attached with an adhesive like wood glue, double sided tape or hot glue.

7. they are fragile, meaning I wouldn't try to nail them to something, maybe with a really thin nail, but I suspect they would split.

8. I am using these for a tiny diorama with tiny toadstools and so far they are so darn cute and just perfect for miniature crafts (FYI I painted this wooden half bead with gouache paint and it turned out exactly the way I hoped it would!).
If it was beads are different from the photo, I think That's not be a sample photo.
Good quality especially for the price. Smaller than I was expecting, so check the measurements. The stickiness is strong for a foam adhesive, in my opinion. Looks exactly as pictured.

The barrel on these markers are Matte compared to regular Ohuhus. The chisel has a finer point but is not any more or less flexible as the Ohuhus regular chisel. The markers bounce back a bit when you put them in their spot in the case which is a pretty cool feature. However performance wise I feel like this is a gimmick only. There is nothing any more different that you can’t do with their cheaper versions of chisel and fine point markers. I love the case though and the illustration is super cute! Ohuhu is the best alternatives to COPIC I have found to date and love the brand, however I’m not convinced!
This is pure disappointment!  It is absolutely nothing like the picture.  Not even remotely.  I know each package is different but this is tiny little pieces of non pattern paper.  Rubbish.

The price and colors are great, but the tape simply doesn't stay stuck to brown cardboard. It came off within 24 hours in our apartment. I had to use staples with it in order to color code our moving boxes.
This is basically paper just a small amount thicker. Definitely would not class as cardstock at all.

The chains are sturdy and beautiful when they came.

The thing that was a problem for me was I wore it with a black Tahitian pearl pendant to a graduation party and by the end of the night, the chain was almost black (the same color as the pearl!) I purchased the silver plated chains and I’m not sure if it was just my skin type that made it change and look tarnished. I ended up throwing that chain away and have not used any others since. I’m not sure what I’m going to do with the rest of them. Don’t even want to give them away if the same thing happens. Definitely would not purchase them again.

Came in a dirty clear bag, looks like someone with glue on their fingers. The size measured 3/4" not 1.5". Quite a few in the bag were bent, way too small to do much with and with this kind of service I would be very skeptical if it was 925. Returned.

Nice but no blue fish.
I DON'T THINK THAT A 1.99 WILL DO IT WHEN I'D PAY 6.99
Good quality for the price.

my first needlepoint project : color on fabric needs to have more degree of variance as some of the  areas are difficult to distinguish from the others and/ or have a cross stitch like paper pattern to follow along with. colors are vibrant and a fairly quick project to complete
There is no way to tell what each needle is.  The package shows what needles are included, but there’s no way to tell which is which.
Really nice brush markers. Good color flow and can be water down with a water brush. Use on watercolor paper or heavy paper. My only complaint is the tip is super pointy. Some hairs came out.
So scratched up when I received it it was useless.

If you are in the mood to be creative and you have time to watch origami videos you can make some fun creatures and flowers pretty easily. This paper is the perfect size for this craft. I made a jumping frog, butterfly and lotus-like flower. Some of the paper in this package is double sided and some single sided. I preferred using the double sided paper as I didn't have to figure out which side to start from. There are 25 colors, 20 sheets of each. It may take you some time to perfect this craft but once you learn a few easy designs you can make them more easily. Watching videos at youtube is really the way to go. I found using this paper to be a fun experience and the paper is of high quality.

~The Rebecca Review

I received one free box of papers for review purposes.
Does the trick -- but, no metric measurements

These tapes are light in color, which is good for some applications. The tape is sheer and a bit fiddly but ok for bullet journals and the like. There are two wider rolls which wanted to tear unevenly, but I like them anyway.

I started using the mixed media and it is ok, but not that great for watercolor. This paper however works fantastic! I’ve watched enough YouTube video of artists who love and recommend Canson papers and I’m glad I listened to them. I officially have joined their ranks. Thank you for a great product Canson!
Packaged torn open. Part of the pens the clips are broke but the main issue is several pens leaked everywhere and were trash.
Fine other than arriving broken. I don't have an organizer to put all of these pieces in so that's fairly annoying.
Good price and great for organization. Don’t get too rough with it though or it will break
Paper is OK..more a student grade paper.

This paper is poor quality. I've had tracing paper before and this one is sub par. I got it and the pages were wrinkly and bumpy. You can't use that kind of paper for good practice. I returned immediately.
Unless you know how to use these skillfully, they will look no different than Crayola markers
Great for classrooms and/or for households with more than one child. Great quality. You will receive 1 box of 60 glue sticks.

Fun to open and look at the coins. Some of the coins were in poor condition so you really need a magnifying glass. Would have liked a better selection, like some coins from Africa would have been nice. There were too many euro coins. But all in all, well worth the price.
Problems include: you usually don't want a back that is so big; they had a hard time going on my earrings.  I ended up donating my order to Goodwill and buying a different product

Took a while to dry. Moves smoothly,.opaque colors are saturated. I bought trial.size.to try. Dont see myself using these alit as they wear down fast and are pricey
However if u want a bold statement to end your art with these will give it
Just what I needed for extra light on my Juki TL 2010Q.

I don't know who is giving this 5 stars...

This may work with transparent resin. My results are from the white resin.

This comes out lighter than the bottle as a hideous weird blue and I can only get two colors out of this no matter how little blue I add or how much white I add, Both are the weird blue. I can't get light blue no matter what I try..

I've abandoned this products and now only use oil paint to color my resin.

Update: I now use this for light blue by putting the tinest mount possible on a popsicle stick and mixing it in. It comes out a nice blue. I don't like it otherwise. It's probably fine for transparents but I do opaques.

I really needed to fix a scratch on my fridge... this is no special pen sadly, looks just like black paint, doesnt really match appliance sheen. i mean, fridge looks a little better not having a scratch on it, but still looks meh

**edit to add**
Couple months later and the paint is worn off. So really not worth it
Well made. Does what you want. Added to my backpack foe easy entry with gloves. Plus the color makes it easy to find

The box they came in the brushes were all dumped out and not secured in the bamboo. The brush mat itself is good, most all the brushes seem okay, except on the tip of 1 which was broken. Without the tip you can't hang to dry, and the broken piece wasn't anywhere in the bamboo mat or box to even try to fix. I do think you get enough sizes to start with painting. I wish they would take the time to check the quality. Note: The set does come with a big brush not pictured.
Tip of scissors are very dull and doesn’t cut fabric. Have to return

About 9 of them came popped with ink over flowing in the caps. I cleaned most of it off and had been in touch with the seller but I have not heard from them back again. I am never buying from this company again as their company has poor customer service. As for the product they are nice markers however they said acrylic paint markers that are water based so it is quite confusing as these were gifted to my younger sister that is learning about art and how to use them the blending is not the best and the customer service is poor therefore I still prefer the alternative brand for true artists.

I have been looking for safety scissors for a very long time!! These are adorable and will not cut your toddler's fingers! You get three scissors and a bundle of pages to cut. I would highly recommend!
Excellent quality, great sheen, deep colors... I love it. Keep in mind the weight is about 1/3 that of dish-cloth type cotton yarns like the Sugar brand.

This is reasonable quality paint for the price, fairly heavy body, reasonable but not crazy pigmentation. The tubes are tiny, but I expected that, just make sure to check the amount.

However, the threaded tube tops are longer than the inside of the caps - so if you tighten them too hard, the cap splits apart and you can't close them any more. By "too hard," I don't mean really torquing them down, just regular pressure. So if you get these, be very careful when closing them. My white was the first casualty, then black went even though I was gentle.

They're cheap, the paint isn't bad. You just have to be ridiculously delicate when closing them (or good at making little aluminum foil caps).

Yes, they will "work" if you're finicky, but even then half of them will smash up into un-usable shapes that couldn't possibly snap into anything, because the metal is just too cheap -- it bends up too easily. If you do manage to get everything to work properly (and this takes a lot of effort, all the more annoying because you're thinking the whole time how stupidly simple it is), the "ideal" snap is still too weak!

Altogether, it's junk.
All 3 leak

Worth the price and can save you lots of money by preventing a lost of an expensive earrings . It's a must for fish hook earrings !
It is amazing to me why such a thing not always included with some fish hook style earrings !
Glad Amazon offers such a variety of items ! Thanks Amazon !

I am not an artist. But I do love crafts and I love painting. This kind of paint was a bit intimidating, but I figured at the very least, I might end up using this set to touch up some old artwork I have. But first I had to try it out and I used the smallest paintbrush that came with the set.

I love this paint and I wish I'd had it all along. Mostly I've used acrylic craft paints, I haven't painted anything too difficult mostly because it just is really hard and I always felt that my dabbling was beyond amateurish, it was sorta ugly, so I stuck to simple stuff; stenciling a box, fixing scratch with some paint, I never did more that wander down the art aisles at Michael's because I wasn't qualified to use anything there.

But this paint is so forgiving and it isn't clumpy like acylic, it is thicker, but more puddingish and you can thin it if you want to layer some colors. All of the colors in this set are metalic and I though it would look good to layer some gold on top of the coppery paint I'd already used.

I should also mention how fast drying this paint is, fast drying could be a blessing or a curse, I mostly think it is amazing, I was able to finish a small artwork in an hour because of this feature. Any mistakes you make are easily remedied because you can simply paint over them!

Something else about the metallic paints in this set, they are really, really metallic. I've bought several metallic acrylic paints and to be honest, they are not very impressive, especially in the darker colors. But each metallic color from copper to pink is shimmery with metallic sparkliness, I love it!

Now for my only misgiving about this set. Each little paint pot has an adhesive cover, the covers are surprisingly difficult to peel back and once you have it is super unclear how you store the unused portion of paint without a replacement cover. I made sure to keep the covers partly attached, but it made it ackward to remove tape while simultaneously holding the lid peeled. Also I do not know if this exposure to the air will fully dry out this paint when I have barely used any, it is a major concern.

It won't turn red, only magenta - no matter how much I use. I see searching google this is common for this product. Epic fail.

This may work with transparent resin. My results are from the white resin.

I've abandoned this products and now only use oil paint to color my resin.

Update: Giving it 3 stars because I can get pink now by using the tiniest amount possible on a popsicle stick and stiring it in. And I meandthe TINIEST amount.

It did the job, I got about 300 6" cuts in thick suede leather out of one blade before I needed a lot of force.

But, it's not a super-quality handle, and not worth $20. It's not falling apart, but it's definitely not better than the other rotary cutters on Amazon for $10.
Some of the product came damaged. Not every piece in every section but a few in each are broken.
It really pretty feels great but comes unwrapped in anything.
Same as headline
Not sturdy. No matter how careful or gently worked, the crimp covers kept breaking.
Arrived dirty and had a number written in pencil probably from the factory. I went to wash it before I put it into the holder and the whole image came off.
These are very poor quality. I will never buy again. At least 25/30% of the pieces are malformed and not useable.

Making fabric bowls is intriguing. I have not made anything directly from this book, but the information is clear and there are a lot of variations to try.  A good book for the reference shelf in my craft room.
Hated it!
Tiny and not worth the price.
I bought these for under my Grandson's Christmas tree. They are really cute and will add to his Polar Express theme. They appear to be as durable as can be expected for the price.
A lot of colors but dries very chalky even with very lite application OK for kids but not for a adult who wants vibrant colors. The paint dries chalky!
It's bad when I get the colors really small high price
Total junk

Cute paper, nice weight, double sided, quality printing BUT it's only 5-7/8" square. For some people, that might not make a difference, but if you're doing projects based off a 6x6" square, you're going to come up short. I had to waste a second sheet to get the number of strips I needed for my layout.

As the other reviewer already noted, this is a nice reference but hardly 'Master classes'. I'll add that there are few charts - even when there should be (lace) and those that are included are so tiny they're almost impossible to see. The print is light, and the font size is 8 or 9 - very difficult to read. I love "Knitter" magazine and had been hoping that this would comparable.

Watercolor
Pictures are not clear & concise. Too many images to watercolor clearly
Very, VERY disappointed in this watercolor paper....will not purchase again and cannot recommend

There is a lot packed into this zippered case, perhaps a little over full. Most of the tools are adequate, similar to what one would find in sets at a craft store. The chain pliers and round nose pliers are pretty good. When I tested the wire cutters on a head pin, they didn’t snap it off, had to bend it slightly to break it. Suppose that they would be ok on thinner wire. All of the rest of the tools lined up ok, and the small size of everything makes it good for travel to craft shows for repair, etc.  There are lots of findings in the plastic compartment case, which is also handy. Probably good for beginners or as an alternate set.
Pretty, but very, very thin, like tissue paper.

First, I ordered the Peacock with the head facing right. I like some things and did not like some things. First I was unhappy with the color of the background. In many pictures it looks pale grey, that's what I was hoping for too, but I got a background that looks too dark and almost like a little grey/mauve tone. It's ugly against the blue peacock. It looks dingy next to the peacocks blue tones. The kit came with all the colors and was numbered okay. I did finish pretty quick because it was only a partial drill, and had plenty of dots. The packets had some random tiny pieces of squares and leftover (too tiny) dots, excess waste mixed in but the worst was some were stuck together and impossible to get apart, that kept happening, so annoying. The background had plenty of tackiness to stick the dots down, and the peacock is pretty when finished. The opening for this is not standard size (19x11.5) not sure I want to pay for custom framing for this. The peacock is pretty some are not bothered by the things that bothered me, but I will probably go with a name brand company if I do another one.
The roller is fine but the picture makes you believe you're going to receive two rollers.
Only kind to buy. Durable and the price was better than the local store.

Amazon offered me lightning deal forfew bucks off Norberg & Linden XXL 125 colored pencils. I never heard of them so I took a chance for another budget friendly set to try out. I was The pencil barrel says the are the Creyarts. If I knew that I would not have bought them. To get the vivid colors I achieved in my diy composition swatch book I had to bear down hard forever and kept getting breakage when I sharpen up the tip. I only colored flowers with them because they did not do well on Amazon ceatespace coloring book paper. I gave up and switched to my Black Widows do rest. I think they are going to OK for my young grandkids when they color with grandma. I do not recommend. The 3 pencils I used for flowers are now much shorter than rest for barely any use

Very dissatisfied with my purchase of the Money and Kadinsky sets . Eight duplicate colors that overlap. I read description and it was not mentioned anywhere that some colors overlap each set . I figured they would be smart and market different colors in the 10 sets so a customer could collect them all . Nope . Not even one or two eight duplicates from just two sets . Cadmium yellow , yellow ochre , flesh deep , purple Lake , mauve , ultramarine , mulberry and Cobalt all were in both sets ! I bought mine  used condition due to damaged tins so I am glad I didn't pay full price. I want to return them just keep classic big set
set was nice and low in price but one was bent and hard to get it straight again
Great price for this bulk pack  Glue sticks are glue sticks. So I was looking for a big pack that the kids could use up or lose without stressing out or running out. Price is right with this pack!

Cute decorative sewing set, with several small tools included in a gift type box.  Since I am very familiar with sewing items I was interested to see this. The needle threaders are cute flower shapes, the thimble is an open top, ring type which is not my preference. The measuring tape is just a flat, white one, not retractable. Round container of a hand sewing needle assortment, standard style. Probably the most interesting items are the scissors, a stork and a unicorn.  They are quite colorful and fun, and good for small projects,  light use. Obviously unusual and perhaps even collectible, or certainly a nice gift set.

PanPastel really needs to work on their outer packaging, it is ineffective for mail-order shipments. My set arrived with Burnt Sienna powder coating EVERYTHING. All of the sponge blenders are already full of Burnt Sienna and the knife tool and each of the other pots of color were coated. What a mess. The way the pots stack and screw into one another is pretty cool, but the "palette tray" is just flimsy molded plastic I don't see any utility in it, including keeping the pigment in place during shipping.
So far I've only tried the Ultramarine Blue to put in a background on a mixed media piece. Blending it on Bristol didn't work too well but I expect it's better on more textured papers.

These really are mediocre. At first these caps looked pretty good, but when I tried using them, the shapes just didn't seem to fit my 6mm beads they just didn't look good - too pointy looking and cheap. You get what you pay for.

The kit came complete with all the colors, the flamingo looks great. The background for the flamingo is white, and because this is a partial drill and it was supposed to look like a watercolor in the background I wasn't sure. Well it's not very good. At the bottom left no dots go there and it's not really legible that it's supposed to be a watercolor/loose style, strange. The pros, everything comes with it, had plenty of dots and all that was supposed to be in the kit. Flamingo is easy to do for a first timer and it was fun. Looks cute when your done. Cons, as I mentioned the non dot areas of the design, also this is an odd size so have to do some calculating for figuring how to frame it.

I purchased all three sets plus both set of skin tones . Love the stunning coordinate colors ! On a y one complaint. I received two Flat Black and missing the Cobra color in the Cobra set . How can I get missing pencil ?

I received these beautiful watercolor tubes today and just LOVE them. A big shout out to all the wonderful artists and crafters that gave such positive reviews for the ShinHan watercolor. I’m primarily a crafter with a lot of projects going at the same time so this is a perfect set for me. The texture and colors are divine. Smooth and silky, bold and vibrant! I can’t believe I’ve been putting up with chalky pans all this time. This set is definitely more bang for the buck. Highly recommended. Thanks for a great product ShinHan!
A few of the colors are duds, but most of them have surprising glitter undertones they don't show up in the pictures. The material is nice and thick. Especially for the price, I am very satisfied.
This is very nice ribbon. The color is pretty true to the picture. Just wish it came in 2" wide size.
Sticks to anything, holds like hell
No.  I would not buy them again.  They just did not fit my hand comfortably.
The pens work well but the colors aren't listed and when I ordered different sets the duplication in pens was 50% ...
It’s a good gift for 8 years old girl

This is a nice addition if you need more room for your fabric. Unfortunately it's a far cry from having a machine level with a sewing table. When you've always had models that sat even with the table rather than on top of the table it's a little awkward. This extension is helpful. If you've always sewed with your machine sitting on a table top you wouldn't need this.

I used this product for diamond painting. It was just the right size for this. The problem was that the little compartment would just pop open on there own and the drills would spill. The compartments weren’t very tight to begin with.
Cheaply made and pins aren’t sharp and won’t pierce fabric. Don’t waste your money. Buy brand name only is my opinion
Doesn’t tighten much so cloth is kind of lose and embroidery has ripples

This book has some pretty examples of art dolls in the gallery.  I got the book for the mermaid pattern, and I do like the shape of the basic doll pattern, it makes a very well-shaped doll.  It is a little tricky to get the darts positioned properly to shape the torso, however.  If this author's books are lacking, it is in instructions.  Two piece legs and arms? How to join the pieces together? It is not explained.  Fortunately, I have made many dolls and do not rely on someone else's directions, but for someone who wants more information, it is not clear.  I know that this book is about beading, and that is pretty and nice, but I do not personally like the beaded face, or understand the need for a beaded tatoo.  All in all, these books are good eye candy and the patterns serve as a jumping off point for creating one's own dolls, and adapting and making some of one's own patterns.
This is probably a good learning book for children. I got it to teach my grandchildren, they took off with it, and I have not seen it since.
Better quality than most lower priced fans- recommend.

So far so good, these are great dots of adhesiveness and I have found so many uses for them around the house;

I'm using one above the light switch so that I don't accidentally flip on the garbage disposal. I was thinking of buying one of those light up lightswitches, but having a scratchy side out dot above lightswitch is doing just as well.

I used a few to hang decorative christmas lights from my fence, outside, they are still there and its been a couple of days now.

I've used two for repairing a christmas wreath and another for fixing a hook in my pantry that was listing sideways.

My next plan is to use it on a shadow box, I think the dots are small enough that they will be unseen from the front or sides of my shadow box.

The possibilities are endless.

I’m just learning tatting and find this product very nice to work with. This Monet colorway is beautiful, and I also really love the Pastels colorway. I’d recommend this product to all Crochet and Tatting crafters. Arrived as promised, and in great condition. I’d purchase from this vendor again.

This is a phenomenal poster that I bought for my son. The colors are vibrant and the characters are like the ones in the comics, creepy and detailed.

It isn't a giant poster, but then again, I'm not sure even my kiddo would like a larger version of this. Some of the lanterns in the poster are downright scary, I would never have wanted a poster like this when I was his age (I had Strawberry shortcake and Holly Hobby). So, for the true lantern corps fans, this is a pretty great picture.

The Sinestro corps and the ultra angry red lantern, fueled by rage, Atroticus, is particularly horrible.

The price is what really got my interest, but it is beyond cool that, this time at least, I got way more than my money's worth when I picked this out.
Not sure if I receive the correct Product. Mine is white, not clear and translucent. It also seems a little bit thicker than normal. Possibly a knock off mat?
Is this a glass bead?  I think it's plastic beads. It looks cheaper than the image, but it's as price.

Opened box...
was showered with hole punch confetti.
Item arrived used.
Upon further examination, I found out the item was defective,
as it was jammed/stuck and I could not insert any paper.
Returning!
I love this brand of pens but save your money as no humans are these colors!

Warning: This was my Christmas gift this morning. The bottles ALL leaked inside and got all over my hands. Really love the gift & am so disappointed that the company doesn’t take returns. I have managed to write to them through Amazon’s  robot but don’t know if they will ever respond -update ! The vendor said it’s UPS fault (zero damage to packaging, all caps off were inside of box) and he’d take it back when he gets it. No address for where to send it, no label to print & UPS delivered it well packaged. Two emails, both says he’s not responsible. We are not wealthy & this was my BIG gift. Disabled, Dr says self isolate (cancer) & so far I wouldn’t know where to send it if my social security could afford it. Just know this was not a “happy moment” like Bob Ross at all.

I'm unable to get the gold and silver lobster clasps to open.  As others have mentioned, the open jump rings are impossible to close correctly.  I bought this to make simple repairs to the jewelry we sell at a thrift store so it will probably fit that need.  You get what you pay for.
Very cheap quality. Not what I expected.

Jessica Smith is my new favorite walking instructor. She has a lot of energy and makes each session of walking fun and entertaining. In this DVD you will find four main workouts, each 15 minutes long.

The Fast Fat Burn is high energy and it whittles the waist. Jessica is giggly and talkative but gets the walking done fast.

Belly Blaster has footwork that is a bit fancy and Jessica's shoes are bright hot pink. The standing crunches in this workout are excellent.

Power Pick Up is energizing and gets the lymph flowing as you are doing a lot of arm raises.

Dance Party doesn't have any complicated steps and is not overly sensual. It is more country music inspired.

With addictive moves and motivating music you will be inspired to do two workouts at a time. This is great if you only have 30 minutes to exercise. Highly Recommended!

~The Rebecca Review

I have tried for three days to make the machine work properly.  I assumed there is a learning curve and I tried over and over again to get it to work well.  Just when I thought it was working properly, it began to drop stitches and finally the entire project - a scarf - only a few inches from being finished - dropped off the entire machine.  Tensioning the yarn is a real problem.  Alignment of the needles is a problem.  Even if they are in the proper position Before the carriage is moved across the yarn, the needles move out of place, the carriage jams and then much time and hand work has to be done to complete the row.  Then, the tension on the stitches is too tight across the row and the next row is difficult to knit. The machine makes a very distressing noise as the it attempts to knit.  It is NOT a relaxing process.  It is not an easy process.  Hand knitting is easier.  I have almost finished ONE project which will cost me the price of the entire machine.  A very expensive and not very exciting scarf.  I am going to put the entire thing away and either throw it out or give it to someone who has more patience than I have.  I wonder if the sliver reed machine is less frustrating and works better than this knitting machine?
Only buy this if you like cheaply made, ugly findings. Wish I could return it. Garbage.
It dries soft and bends with the clothes' takes about 24 hours to dry.
I ordered a size 2-3-4 so I could sew a size 2 shirt for my great grand son. But I received a size 4-5-6! So very disappointed!!! can't use this for several years.
Students love it. Wish they were a bit less expensive.
Too expensive, but worked well for art projects

This is a really great selection of brushes, everything you need to do some fine, detailed painting.

There is even a tiny liner that I think will be great on a mosaic painting I'm planning for the inside of a drawer.

The case is pretty nice, my only complaint is that I have several small zippered cases that are black, I just wish they came in other colors.

The only brush that's missing is a round flat one for stenciling.

I checked all the bristles and none were loose or falling out. So all in all this is a pretty great set.

This is a fantastic product that has been around forever. I use it to glue puzzles and I have found that one coat on each side of the puzzle is more than adequate. For an extra layer of hold or protection two layers of glue on each side will be far more than you need. This is the matte finish which I prefer,  but many other finishes are available from satin to glossy.

This isnt quality watercolor paper. Its decent but not good enough for those of us who layer. It peels badly when you layer it. If you dont plan on laying then this is pretty good. But stay away if you love or need to layer for your art
I saw it, but I wish it was all stainless steel because I live near a beach and the metals turn and tarnish before using them.

I had bought the same brand in 5 lbs so i was hoping the 10 lbs would work out.
It must have been stored improperly or is nearing its expiration maybe?
Compared to the 5 pounder, it didn’t melt well in the microwave, was clumpy, formed films on the surface, formed a lot of clumpy crap when pouring into soap molds, and ugh the smell is awful!!

I purchased the 24 Original Jar Set. The photo shows a little more vibrancy in color than in person, (camera photo). After reading another user had successfully used on polymer clay, I decided to try it and made button testers on plain white Sculpey III. I applied each powder to raw clay and baked at 275° for 30 minutes. When you open the door the fumes were not visible but potent, burns your eyes and heavy smell of burnt crayons, so I had to open windows . So I came back to read, the Rolio seller said they are not to be baked past 248°F. Well that explains the fumes. Should post that in the description...
Most were not that fine, a few had too much glitter, a few were flat, some colors are way to close in tone, almost identical. I tried mixing a couple into the clay, but it just did not have enough pigment or smooth enough to notice any color. Two colors were nice and fine, most were not that great. I wouldn't purchase again, I've worked with finer Mica's and the temp won't work for me doing clay.

I like it. Okay, the big plus is it only takes a bobbin and that threads into the tension then into the needle. You can't use a spool - won't fit on the bobbin peg. Get this - there is NO way to thread the bobbin itself, on it. You have to have a real machine to thread the bobbins for you.

The good news is you never have to put in a bobbin underneath the foot. It only uses the bobbin on top to feeding the thread to the needle. All that grief I kept getting from my expensive Singer that I finally threw out because I was sick of fighting with it, is gone. I don't have to fight with the jammed bobbin under the foot because that doesn't exist on this. I can just quickly thread and sew in a straight line.

- It doesn't go backwards. you have to stop it- put the needle down and turn the material around to get it to go in the opposite direction. You need to do this to start and stop or the material will pull apart a few inches after where you started and finished.
- to insert fabric you have to unplug it from the battery(or you'll bump the on switch and it will sew), then you manually pick up the metal part under the foot and slide fabric in and out.
- It is consistently tight when you stop like it's jammed - you can't pull your fabric out and have trailing thread like you do on real machines. You have to unplug it from the battery, lift up metal piece under the foot and pull the material hard enough to free it and take your fabric out. That's everytime I stop. But it comes out, the needle stays threaded and I can start again with no fuss on the next thing I want to quickly sew.
- it takes 4 double A batteries and I see no wall power for it. Batteries are expensive.

This is not for any real sewing. It's hand held. It's only for quick hems. For what it is, I like it and will use it.

Update: I no longer use this and I suggest you don't either. for the same price you can get a Lil Sew and Sew and nearly have an actual machine. I'm happy with my $17 Sew and Sew but I'm going for a Brother XR3774 now because I want a stable machine real machine.

I've now painted all of the sheets of this watercolor pad, some on both sides. The paper itself is not super high quality; water tends to puddle, it buckles some even when glued to the pad, and masking fluid can't be removed without tearing the surface. It's also impossible to get all the little glue bits neatly off the edges of the piece when it's removed from the block. Even by inexpensive paper standards, it's only okay: the Canson watercolor pad is much sturdier.

That said, the shape is fun, it was cheap, and I had fun using it to essentially play and doodle with pieces I knew I wasn't going to care too much about. I bought it because I wasn't sure I would ever use round paper, and I definitely do reach for it. It's not like it's unusable--the absorbency is okay and it doesn't buckle badly. I just wouldn't plan to use it for anything you're hoping to treasure, or anything too technically demanding.

I bought a package of wooden beads several years ago and about a 1/4 of them were damaged, usually the hole in the middle didn't go all the way through so I had to carve it out myself to make them usable, but almost all of these are perfect and, as a Bonus, they are all primed with a layer of white paint.

The paint is thin, it looks like the old fashioned milk paint type, so if you are into that shabby chic thing, you could leave these undecorated. But for me, it just means I can use less paint on each ball, yay!

So I am painting these and using some pipe cleaners and a small block of florist foam to hold them while they dry. I am using cheap acrylic paint, the kind that costs under a dollar per bottle and comes in like a million shades.

The balls come with two wodden tags that say Home Sweet Home, so cheesy, I will probably paint over these and use them as garden tags if I get my act together and plant something this pring.

There are several bundles of twine that could be used to make a garland, if you have trouble threading the balls, tie a piece of twine onto a wire twisty tie (like the kind that come with produce bags) or with a toothpick.

Since you can use less paint that means you have a shorter dry time, I don't know if everyone else likes instant craft results like me, but this has turned into a 2hour craft, yahoo!

The balls, tags and twine arrive in a decent sized ziploc, I am storing the extras in this, no reason for them to get lost in the bottom of my sewing bag.

All in all, these are a great crafting item.

I deleted my previous review on this. Mine works but had tension problems out of the box. Still I bought another sewing machine and didn't take it out of the box for months because I kept using this. I like the size! I don't like the tension needing constant adjusting.

- it didn't have a light - I taped a dollar store led light on it.
- it doesn't have reverse - you have to stop the machine, put the needle down and turn the fabric around. (Always start sewing and afer 1/2 inch go in reverse then after 1/2 inch go forward again to lock you stitches at beginning and end of your sewing.)

However, it works for me and I've sewn a lot on it. I recently bought a Brother XR3774 for $118 that sews perfectly and has no tension problems. But it's a boat. The Lil Sew and Sew is tiny and easier to grab. I finally pulled out the Mint Janome because it's smaller than the Brother and tried it - I'll use it since it's smaller. But I really like the size of this sew and sew which is the much smaller and lighter of the 3.

Crazy as it seems, I've considered spinning the roulette wheel for $15 bucks on this and buying another and hopping for no tension problems - because this is so utterly light and easy to use. Update: I'm not going back to it. My mint Janome is my main machine because of size and my Brother is hanging around with black thread in it to use instead of re-threading the Janome when I need black. If you don't have money, fine buy this cheapy but get ready to learn to adjust tension every foot you sew. It has no light and no reverse. The foot pedal is so small I used it as a hand pedal and placed it next to the machine. I did sew a whole lot with this and I adored it until I got my far better machines that require no tension adjusting, have reverse etc. If you can, spend the extra - ear mark your next gob of cash - pay check etc. to buy a decent machine and pick something that gave 5 stars in 90 percent of the reviews. This one is spinning a roulette wheel and doesn't work for half of reviewers.

When I was on the design team of a rubber stamp/scrapbooking store, this is the kind of product that they would hand me, and ask that I make samples and show customers how to use the item.  At first appearance, it seems that there are gears that turn and somehow interlock.  This is not the case.  The gears are positioning devices to place on paper in order to stamp in a circle or oval.  The first concern, however, is the packaging.  As others have mentioned, much of the inspriation and instruction is on the fold out package. The plastic bubbles that enclose the gears and stamps are not easy to remove without destroying the package.  I ended up cutting the plastic bubbles off with an exacto knife, keeping the packaging intact.  I then had to use another container to store the stamps and gears in.  Back to the product.  I would recommend that a couple of small register marks are made in pencil at the arrows on the gears in case they slip a little. They do have a non-skid type of bottom, but if they move even slightly, it can cause problems with lining up the design. I did find that using the same stamp all the way around was ok, but a little boring. The stamps are largely floral, and somewhat Victorian.  It is necessary to ink the stamp between impressions if a consistent color is desired.  I used them with chalk inks in various pastels. It is possible to vary the design by using alternating stamps. By way of explanation, the cling stamps are placed on a holder, and there is a point on either end of that holder which fits into the cogs.  According to the instructions, all can be used interchangeably, and that is another reason to make a small register mark, in case one wants to position another element. All in all, an interesting product, all of the stamps can be used independently of this system, and it is possible to use the positioning cogs for partial symmetry, as desired by the consumer.  It seems to be well made, the stamps cling well, and the instructions are pretty good, all parts are easy to clean. Intriguing.

I bought these to get started with Chinese/Japanese brush painting but I think I’ll just continue to use them. Great for watercolor too. Hard to believe some of the negative reviews. I particularly enjoyed the positive comments from the seasoned artists. Those comments are what made me go ahead with purchasing these brushes.

I am using these brushes for some stencils I got for an old cabinet. I have wanted a set of brushes like this for a long time, but they were always kind pricey compared to the sponge topped spouncers (I don't know if that is what they were called or if I have just invented a word based on a sound) I usually get when I have some stencilling or a paint effect to create.

But these brushes are so nice and I have a lot of stenciling to do, so I figured I really needed them They do work very much like 'spouncers' except that you can get a lot more interesting texture with these brushes, which is great for the ravens I have been stenciling, it gives them a wilder look.

Something else to know is that these brushes are a lot easier to clean. Sponges take a lot of effort to clean and they don't last very long if you are planning on cleaning them after each color coat.

To sum up, these are great. They also have had no shedding (so far, all brushes shed) and I think that is serious clue that these are well made and will last a while if I take care of them.

If you have never made soaps before this soap base is a good base to start with. I cut the bar in half and then cut the half into two more places to make three small pieces of soap. Then I melted them in a double boiler using a spoon to stir everything around and around until it all melted. Then I added in rose essential oil and two drops red food coloring. Once everything was all mixed up I poured the melted soap base into flower soap molds. One half of the soap base makes enough for one Something Fabulous soap mold. So I made six flower soaps and I poured the rest into a plastic bag which I set in a stainless bowl. So half this bar made seven soaps. Getting the soaps out of the molds is the hard part. I'm going to look for silicone molds in the future. But using the soaps was a pure joy. The lather is super foamy and the feeling is that your skin is left with a protective layer of moisture. I'll definitely buy this soap base again. I loved the whole experience of making my own soaps and this really is the easy way to go. I can see myself making more soaps as gifts for Christmas. You can color this any way you want to and put whatever essential oils you like in it. A great experience!

~The Rebecca Review
